_**Agent (default)**_

Your local `main` and `origin/main` have **diverged**, so Git blocks sync (push) with a non-fast-forward error.

## What happened

After the phase-4 merge (`24c8259`), two things happened in parallel:

**On your machine (local `main`):**
1. `80042df` — APP-OVERVIEW docs & screenshots  
2. `6d9d697` — ROADMAP / phase documentation update (your latest commit)

**On GitHub (`origin/main`):**
1. `9dcb8d9` — Phase 2.1 intake corrections  
2. `b75e553` — Merge PR #6 (`phase-2.1`)

So you're **ahead by 1** commit and **behind by 2**. Git won't push because remote has commits you don't have locally.

```
        6d9d697  ← your ROADMAP commit (local only)
       /
24c8259
       \
        b75e553  ← phase-2.1 merge (remote only)
```

Merging `main` locally only helps if you had the **latest** `origin/main`. Phase 2.1 was merged on GitHub after your local branch split off, so your local `main` is stale relative to remote.

## How to fix

Integrate remote changes, then push:

**Option A — rebase (linear history, usually preferred):**
```bash
git pull --rebase origin main
git push origin main
```

**Option B — merge (adds a merge commit):**
```bash
git pull origin main
git push origin main
```

Both should work here since your ROADMAP commit is docs-only and unlikely to conflict with phase-2.1 code.

If Cursor shows "Sync" greyed out or failing, this divergence is the reason — pull/rebase first, then push.

Want me to run the rebase and push for you?

_**Agent (default)**_

Fixed and synced.

The conflict was in `docs/phases/README.md`: remote had **Phase 2.1 = Complete**, your commit had **Phase 3 = Complete**, and each side still had the other phase as Planned.

**Resolution applied:**
- Phase 2.1 → Complete (from the merged PR)
- Phase 3 → Complete (from your docs update)
- Updated the progress table in `docs/ROADMAP.md` so Phase 2.1 is also marked complete there

The rebase finished and `main` was pushed to GitHub. Your branch is now in sync with `origin/main`. Sync in Cursor should work again.
